purpose: Core scenario library for the Mono & DIV Economy Game.
principle: Scenarios are structured shocks. They should affect demand, adoption, treasury execution, liquidity, confidence, fiat usefulness, regulation, and price formation through explicit variables.
engineNote: V1 currently has a smaller embedded scenario library inside scenarioShockEngine.js. This file is the larger data-driven scenario library intended to replace that embedded library later.

scenarios.name: Viral promotion wave
scenarios.explanation: A viral attention wave increased public interest, but not all of the demand is high quality.

scenarios.name: Major creator endorsement
scenarios.explanation: A major endorsement increased attention and demand, especially for DIV.

scenarios.name: Search interest spike
scenarios.explanation: Search interest rose sharply, increasing awareness before durable adoption could catch up.

scenarios.name: Documentary feature
scenarios.explanation: A serious documentary feature improved awareness and made demand slightly more durable.

scenarios.name: Media backlash
scenarios.explanation: Negative media coverage raised attention but reduced trust and increased sell pressure.

scenarios.name: Influencer misinformation cycle
scenarios.explanation: Misleading public claims created attention, confusion, speculation, and volatility.

scenarios.name: Mainstream news segment
scenarios.explanation: Mainstream coverage increased visibility and brought new buyers into the market.

scenarios.name: Viral criticism thread
scenarios.explanation: A viral criticism thread briefly damaged sentiment and pushed weaker holders to sell.

scenarios.name: Education campaign success
scenarios.explanation: A clear education campaign improved higher-quality adoption instead of pure hype.

scenarios.name: Attention fades
scenarios.explanation: Public attention faded, reducing speculative demand and slowing new user growth.

scenarios.name: Payment adoption breakthrough
scenarios.explanation: A payment-use breakthrough improved durable Mono adoption.

scenarios.name: Merchant integration delay
scenarios.explanation: Merchant integration delays slowed high-quality adoption.

scenarios.name: Business settlement pilot
scenarios.explanation: A business settlement pilot improved Mono's real economic usefulness.

scenarios.name: Institutional trial program
scenarios.explanation: Institutional trials increased credibility but also attracted more scrutiny.

scenarios.name: User churn wave
scenarios.explanation: User churn increased because the economy failed to convert attention into durable utility.

scenarios.name: Merchant coverage accelerates
scenarios.explanation: Merchant coverage improved, making Mono more useful as a payment asset.

scenarios.name: Merchant coverage lag
scenarios.explanation: User demand grew faster than merchant coverage, weakening payment adoption quality.

scenarios.name: Sticky user growth
scenarios.explanation: More users became sticky, improving the quality of Mono adoption.

scenarios.name: Business confidence declines
scenarios.explanation: Businesses became more cautious, slowing settlement adoption and weakening trust.

scenarios.name: Adoption overload
scenarios.explanation: Adoption grew faster than liquidity, merchant coverage, and support infrastructure.

scenarios.name: DIV dividend hype cycle
scenarios.explanation: Dividend expectations created a demand cycle for DIV.

scenarios.name: Dividend sustainability skepticism
scenarios.explanation: The market questioned whether DIV dividends are sustainable.

scenarios.name: Missed dividend expectation
scenarios.explanation: DIV holders expected a stronger dividend than the treasury could credibly deliver.

scenarios.name: Dividend credibility rally
scenarios.explanation: A credible dividend history improved DIV trust and demand quality.

scenarios.name: Speculative dividend bubble
scenarios.explanation: Speculation around DIV dividends became detached from sustainable treasury capacity.

scenarios.name: Dividend policy clarity
scenarios.explanation: Clear dividend rules reduced uncertainty and improved policy trust.

scenarios.name: Dividend policy confusion
scenarios.explanation: Unclear dividend policy made DIV holders doubt the treasury's long-term plan.

scenarios.name: Dividend seekers rotate in
scenarios.explanation: Dividend-seeking buyers increased DIV demand.

scenarios.name: Dividend seekers rotate out
scenarios.explanation: Dividend-focused holders reduced exposure, increasing DIV sell pressure.

scenarios.name: Unsustainable dividend expectations
scenarios.explanation: DIV dividend expectations became too high relative to treasury capacity.

scenarios.name: Mono inventory stress
scenarios.explanation: Mono treasury inventory fell below safe control levels.

scenarios.name: DIV inventory stress
scenarios.explanation: DIV treasury inventory fell below safe control levels.

scenarios.name: Mono support test
scenarios.explanation: Mono traded below the treasury buy point, forcing the market to test support.

scenarios.name: Treasury credibility rally
scenarios.explanation: The treasury successfully defended policy and improved credibility.

scenarios.name: Failed support event
scenarios.explanation: The market saw the treasury fail to fully defend support, damaging credibility.

scenarios.name: Spread too tight
scenarios.explanation: Treasury spreads were too tight for current volatility, increasing arbitrage vulnerability.

scenarios.name: Treasury overexpansion
scenarios.explanation: The treasury expanded supply faster than inventory and trust could support.

scenarios.name: Treasury conservative policy
scenarios.explanation: A conservative treasury stance improved credibility and reserve confidence.

scenarios.name: Fiat support capacity questioned
scenarios.explanation: The market questioned whether fiat reserves were liquid enough to defend policy.

scenarios.name: Inventory transparency boost
scenarios.explanation: Clear treasury inventory reporting improved reserve confidence.

scenarios.name: Liquidity provider arrival
scenarios.explanation: New liquidity providers improved market depth and reduced volatility.

scenarios.name: Whale sell event
scenarios.explanation: A large holder created sudden sell pressure and tested treasury support.

scenarios.name: Exchange outage
scenarios.explanation: An exchange outage reduced liquidity and made price discovery unstable.

scenarios.name: Arbitrage attack
scenarios.explanation: Arbitrageurs exploited weak spreads, low liquidity, or treasury mispricing.

scenarios.name: Market depth collapse
scenarios.explanation: Market depth collapsed, making even normal order flow move prices violently.

scenarios.name: Liquidity fragmentation
scenarios.explanation: Liquidity split across venues, weakening execution quality and price stability.

scenarios.name: Market maker confidence
scenarios.explanation: Market makers improved depth, reducing volatility and strengthening liquidity trust.

scenarios.name: Volatility cluster
scenarios.explanation: Recent instability created more instability, producing volatility clustering.

scenarios.name: Thin weekend liquidity
scenarios.explanation: Temporary thin liquidity made prices more sensitive to small flows.

scenarios.name: Order book improves
scenarios.explanation: The order book deepened, making treasury policy easier to execute.

scenarios.name: Risk-on macro environment
scenarios.explanation: A risk-on environment increased appetite for growth and speculative assets.

scenarios.name: Recession fear
scenarios.explanation: Recession fear pushed the market away from risky assets and tested DIV.

scenarios.name: Credit tightening
scenarios.explanation: Tighter credit reduced speculative appetite and weakened market liquidity.

scenarios.name: Growth boom
scenarios.explanation: A broader growth boom supported demand, adoption, and risk appetite.

scenarios.name: Global slowdown
scenarios.explanation: A global slowdown weakened risk assets and reduced liquidity.

scenarios.name: Safe-haven rotation
scenarios.explanation: Investors rotated toward stability, helping Mono more than DIV.

scenarios.name: Speculative rotation
scenarios.explanation: Speculative capital rotated into growth assets, boosting DIV but lowering demand quality.

scenarios.name: Risk-off liquidity freeze
scenarios.explanation: Risk-off conditions froze liquidity and amplified sell pressure.

scenarios.name: Macro confidence recovery
scenarios.explanation: Macro confidence recovered, improving liquidity and risk appetite.

scenarios.name: Asset price correction
scenarios.explanation: A broader asset correction reduced speculative demand and increased sell pressure.

scenarios.name: Inflation spike
scenarios.explanation: Inflation fear weakened fiat usefulness and increased monetary-alternative demand.

scenarios.name: Currency devaluation scare
scenarios.explanation: Currency weakness raised demand for alternatives but damaged fiat reserve quality.

scenarios.name: High-yield currency trap
scenarios.explanation: High nominal yield was offset by FX decay, inflation, and liquidity risk.

scenarios.name: Real fiat return collapse
scenarios.explanation: Fiat reserves started losing real purchasing power faster than nominal yield could compensate.

scenarios.name: Fiat usefulness decline
scenarios.explanation: Fiat became less useful as a store, reserve, or support asset.

scenarios.name: Safe fiat yield window
scenarios.explanation: Safe fiat yields improved treasury reserve quality.

scenarios.name: Global FX volatility
scenarios.explanation: FX volatility made the treasury fiat basket less predictable.

scenarios.name: Reserve currency strength
scenarios.explanation: Major reserve currencies strengthened, improving treasury fiat usefulness.

scenarios.name: Fiat saturation warning
scenarios.explanation: Treasury fiat grew large enough that marginal reserves became less useful.

scenarios.name: Fiat reserve quality stress
scenarios.explanation: The fiat reserve basket became less useful for defending the system.

scenarios.name: Regulatory warning
scenarios.explanation: Regulatory warnings reduced confidence and increased sell pressure.

scenarios.name: Banking access restriction
scenarios.explanation: Banking restrictions made fiat ramps and treasury support harder to use.

scenarios.name: Capital control pressure
scenarios.explanation: Capital-control pressure reduced the treasury's ability to move and deploy fiat reserves.

scenarios.name: Tax reporting shock
scenarios.explanation: New reporting pressure made some users and businesses less willing to adopt.

scenarios.name: Government resistance escalation
scenarios.explanation: The system became large enough to trigger stronger political resistance.

scenarios.name: Regulatory clarity positive
scenarios.explanation: Clearer rules improved institutional comfort and adoption trust.

scenarios.name: Legal uncertainty
scenarios.explanation: Legal uncertainty slowed business and institutional adoption.

scenarios.name: Exchange compliance review
scenarios.explanation: Exchange compliance checks temporarily reduced liquidity and confidence.

scenarios.name: Policy misinterpretation
scenarios.explanation: The market misread a policy signal, briefly increasing uncertainty.

scenarios.name: Political acceptance signal
scenarios.explanation: A political acceptance signal improved regulatory survival trust.

scenarios.name: Banking stress
scenarios.explanation: Banking stress increased interest in Mono but reduced normal fiat liquidity.

scenarios.name: Settlement delay
scenarios.explanation: Fiat settlement delays made treasury support less immediate.

scenarios.name: Credit market shock
scenarios.explanation: Credit stress reduced liquidity and weakened speculative demand.

scenarios.name: Reserve access disruption
scenarios.explanation: The treasury's fiat reserves became harder to access under stress.

scenarios.name: Bank run contagion
scenarios.explanation: Traditional banking panic spread, increasing Mono demand but damaging fiat support infrastructure.

scenarios.name: Banking reliability improves
scenarios.explanation: Banking reliability improved, strengthening fiat support capacity.

scenarios.name: Payment rail outage
scenarios.explanation: Traditional payment rails failed temporarily, making Mono more useful for payments.

scenarios.name: Reserve audit delay
scenarios.explanation: Delayed reserve transparency made the market question treasury strength.

scenarios.name: Liquid support shortage
scenarios.explanation: The treasury had fiat on paper but not enough liquid support capacity.

scenarios.name: Banking partner added
scenarios.explanation: A stronger banking relationship improved fiat deployment and institutional comfort.

scenarios.name: Strong competitor launch
scenarios.explanation: A competing system reduced attention and increased churn.

scenarios.name: Technology upgrade success
scenarios.explanation: A successful technology upgrade improved trust and adoption quality.

scenarios.name: Reliability issue
scenarios.explanation: Reliability problems made users and businesses less willing to depend on Mono or DIV.

scenarios.name: Security scare
scenarios.explanation: A security scare damaged trust and triggered broad sell pressure.

scenarios.name: Developer ecosystem growth
scenarios.explanation: Developer activity improved the ecosystem and supported higher-quality adoption.

scenarios.name: Integration tooling failure
scenarios.explanation: Integration tooling problems slowed merchant and business adoption.

scenarios.name: User experience improves
scenarios.explanation: A better user experience improved conversion and reduced churn.

scenarios.name: Competitor scandal
scenarios.explanation: A competitor scandal pushed some attention and demand toward Mono and DIV.

scenarios.name: Technology roadmap doubt
scenarios.explanation: Doubts about the technology roadmap slowed adoption and weakened demand.

scenarios.name: Infrastructure scaling success
scenarios.explanation: Infrastructure scaled successfully, improving adoption quality and system credibility.
